Since both fuzzers already have I2S, the differentiator is the CMP_MAP gradient: it gives partial-credit feedback on the byte-by-byte prefix/URI string comparisons (e.g. the prefix == pref pointer/identity and the surrounding namespace-name comparisons) so value_profile_cmplog climbs incrementally toward valid namespaced markup that cmplog's all-or-nothing edge signal cannot bridge.

what_input_feature: A DTD whose internal subset opens with '<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "..."' followed by a '[' and is then driven far enough that xmlParseLookupInternalSubset locates the closing ']' / '>' delimiter of the subset; the parser must be in XML_PARSER_DTD state with a lookahead-complete internal subset, not merely an opened-but-unterminated one.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog seeds carry well-formed DTD prologs ('<?xml version="1.0"?>' then '<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1...' / 'dtd://') and build up richer subset bodies (xlink namespace strings, multiple declarations), so the buffered lookahead reaches the internal-subset terminator and xmlParseLookupInternalSubset returns true, taking the false branch into xmlParseInternalSubset.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog seeds stall on near-miss DTD bytes ('dtds~1', 'S[STEM', 'DOCTYPE\n', 'dt.s=1') that either corrupt the DOCTYPE keyword or never complete the internal subset, so xmlParseLookupInternalSubset keeps returning 0 (true branch, goto done) and the parser re-enters the loop without progressing.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient feedback on top of the I2S that both fuzzers already share; the gradient rewards inputs that get incrementally closer to satisfying the byte-comparison-driven lookahead inside xmlParseLookupInternalSubset, giving the winner a smooth path to a complete internal subset that cmplog (edge-bucket feedback only) cannot climb.

what_input_feature: An XML document with a DTD (DOCTYPE/SYSTEM) and a namespace declaration (xmlns:prefix) whose attribute name is NOT declared in the DTD's attribute table, so the xmlGetDtdQAttrDesc/xmlGetDtdAttrDesc lookups all return NULL and xmlValidateOneNamespace sees attrDecl == NULL. Reaching the validation call first requires passing the byte-exact 'xmlns' prefix comparison cascade (att[0..4] == 'x','m','l','n','s') in the SAX2 attribute path.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves the branch in 8/10 trials. Its winning seeds carry a DOCTYPE/SYSTEM DTD and reach xmlValidateOneNamespace with attrDecl NULL: on the d=1 chain the blocker line L4660 shows W taking both sides (T=9 F=9) while the loser only ever takes the false side (T=0 F=27). The CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards partial matches on the multi-byte 'xmlns' equality cascades, letting the winner climb att[0]=='x'..att[4]=='s' (L1235/L1236, L1724/L1725) which W hits (T=18 F=0 / T=9 F=0) but the loser stalls on (T=6 F=6, T=0 F=0).
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolves the branch in 0/10 trials, blocked in 8. cmplog has the same I2S mutator but lacks value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient, so it cannot accumulate partial-credit feedback on the conjunctive 'xmlns' byte comparisons: at L1724 the loser is T=0 F=0 (never reaches the test true) and at L1235 it is split T=6 F=6, never driving the namespace-validation call where attrDecl == NULL can be observed taking its true side (L4660 loser T=0 F=27).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets on intercepted CMPs) is the single technique delta between value_profile_cmplog (winner) and cmplog (loser); both already have the same input-to-state substitution mutator, so that comparison-solving capability is held constant. The gradient gives partial-match feedback on the multi-byte 'xmlns' equality cascade, letting the winner assemble the namespace-decl path that the loser cannot.
